changes.description: Added: (1) DAT_DIR/DAT_EXTRACTOR/DAT_RUNNER/DAT_REPORT constants; (2) run_doc_as_test_check() that extracts+runs tsc, categorizes failures by TS2xxx (blocking) vs TS1xxx-only (warning), handles crash gracefully; (3) updated print_summary(delta, dat) to show both gates in a combined display; (4) updated main() to run doc-as-test after regex drift, combine verdict (regression OR blocking_failures → exit 1); (5) updated --quiet mode output to include dat_passed/dat_skipped/dat_blocking/dat_warnings/verdict

Added 'Doc-as-test layer' section covering: what it catches vs regex validator, gate policy (TS2xxx blocks / TS1xxx warns), opt-out per-block with skip marker, current scope (TS only / top 28 pages / candidate only), local run instructions

notes.quiet_mode_output: docs-ops drift: baseline=0 candidate=0 delta=0 new_pairs=0 dat_passed=72 dat_skipped=4 dat_blocking=0 dat_warnings=0 verdict=PASS
notes.blocking_scenario: If a block contains 'error TS2339: Property foo does not exist on type AmityChannel', run_doc_as_test_check() puts it in blocking_failures. print_summary prints '✗ DOC-AS-TEST TYPE ERRORS (BLOCKING):' section, main() returns exit 1. The quiet line would show dat_blocking=1 verdict=FAIL.
notes.crash_handling: If extract-blocks.py or run-tests.py crashes (CalledProcessError), the gate emits a warning '⚠ runner crashed (non-blocking)' and continues. The overall verdict is NOT affected by a runner crash — infra failures don't block pushes.
notes.design_rationale: TS2xxx-only blocking policy keeps false-positive rate near zero. TS1xxx errors (parser-only) are common in illustrative/partial code examples and should never block — they show up as warnings. This matches the 0049 skip-marker philosophy: intentional pseudocode gets a skip marker; accidental type errors get caught by the gate.
